title: Regulations for the Control of Lasers

applicability.description: Applies to all persons who receive, possess, use, transfer, own, acquire, operate, maintain, repair or service a laser

key_definitions.Annual: Period not exceeding 365 days (366 in leap year)
key_definitions.Applicant: Includes shareholders 5%+, officers, directors, partners, trustees
key_definitions.Health_Care_Laser_Facility: Facility using laser for diagnostic, therapeutic or medical research
key_definitions.Inspection: Official examination including tests, surveys, monitoring
key_definitions.Laser: Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation
key_definitions.Laser_Facility: Location with one or more lasers under same administrative control
key_definitions.Laser_Safety_Officer: Individual designated by employer to perform LSO duties
key_definitions.Mobile_Laser: Laser unit transported to site for operations
key_definitions.Registrant: Person registered with Radiation Control Program

121_005_all_lasers.description: All persons installing, operating, using, maintaining, repairing or servicing lasers
121_005_all_lasers.responsibility: Owner and operator ensure facility meets ANSI Z136.1

121_006_optical_fiber.description: Optical fiber communication systems with laser or LED sources

121_007_health_care.description: Lasers in health care facilities for diagnostic/therapeutic/medical research

requirements: Laser meets design and labeling requirements per ANSI Z136.1
Laser radiation cannot exceed Class 1 limits in audience areas
Mirror balls/flat mirrors require scanning safeguards
Performers viewing beam: cannot exceed Class 1; when not viewing: cannot exceed Class 2
Unattended lasers (Class 2+): 20 feet above OR 8 feet side/below audience floor
Continuous operator control (Class 2+): 10 feet above OR 8 feet side/below audience floor
Designated laser safety officer responsible for shutdown if unsafe
FDA variance required per 21 CFR Ch. I, Subchapter J, Part 1040
FAA notification copy submitted to Radiation Control Program

registration.facility_definition: Facility with Class 3B or Class 4 lasers under same administrative control
registration.exemption: Lasers in transit or storage incident thereto

certificate.posting: Current certificate or legible copy posted conspicuously
certificate.modification: Director may incorporate additional requirements for public protection

renewal.grace_period: Application filed 30+ days prior to expiration extends validity until determination

change_notification.requirement: Notify in writing before changes rendering application info inaccurate
change_notification.disposition: Include recipient when disposing of laser device

vendor_requirements.trigger: Sell, lease, transfer, lend, dispose, assemble, or install Class 3B or Class 4 laser
required_info: Manufacturer, model, serial number, class of each device

state_laser_facilities_temporary_use.notice_requirement: 10 working days before use in Commonwealth
compliance_requirements: Comply with all applicable Radiation Control Program regulations
Register laser(s) with Radiation Control Program
state_laser_facilities_temporary_use.inspection: Operational inspection may be required at Director's discretion

entertainment_additional_requirements.notification_time: Within 10 days of first scheduled performance
required_info: Name, address, phone of laser owner
Name, address, phone of laser safety officer
Name, address, phone of laser operator(s)
Name, address, phone of individual in charge of performance
Length of time laser in operation
Class of laser and manufacturer name
FDA variance and accession number and date

record_keeping.class_3b_4_records: Receipt, transfer, and disposal records for all Class 3B and Class 4 lasers
record_keeping.ansi_requirements: Plus ANSI Z136.1, Z136.2, Z136.3 recordkeeping requirements

inspection_rights: Registrant shall afford opportunity to inspect laser devices and premises
Records maintained pursuant to regulations available for inspection upon reasonable notice

waiver_provisions.authority: Commissioner or designee may waive requirements
findings_required: Substantial compliance with spirit of requirement
Compliance does not jeopardize health or safety

enforcement.purpose: Promote and protect health and safety of public and employees

civil_penalties.assessment: Whether or not violation was willful
factors: Actual and potential danger to public health/environment

grounds_for_suspension: Public health, safety, or environment threatened by delay
grounds_for_denial_revocation: Failed to pay registration fee or civil penalties
Failed to allow inspections or impeded enforcement
Endangered public health, safety, or environment
Used laser for purposes other than registration

contact_info.address: Radiation Control Program, State Laboratory, 305 South Street, Jamaica Plain, MA 02130
